question: [File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by choosing a dust-sized units value, cause tickToPrice(offer.tick) to produce a buyerPrice and sellerPrice whose mulDivDown/mulDivUp results are equal (buyerAssets == sellerAssets), so that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] receives zero increment despite a nonzero _settlementFee, violating the invariant that every take with nonzero settlementFee must accrue a positive claimableSettlementFee? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, WAD/buyerPrice - 1] for a market with nonzero settlementFeeCbp and assert claimableSettlementFee delta > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker repeatedly execute minimum-unit takes on a buy offer (offer.buy=true) where units * buyerPrice / WAD and units * sellerPrice / WAD both floor to the same integer, accumulating zero settlement fee across N takes while consuming the full offer capacity, violating the invariant that total claimableSettlementFee accrued equals sum(buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) > 0? Proof idea: loop N small takes, assert sum of claimableSettlementFee deltas equals expected units_total * settlementFee / WAD.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / settlementFee] Can a taker exploit the PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP=1e12 quantization in tickToPrice to select a tick where offerPrice is rounded down by up to 1e12-1, causing the effective settlement fee spread (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) to be systematically lower than units * _settlementFee / WAD, underclaiming protocol fees? Proof idea: for each tick in [0, MAX_TICK], compute tickToPrice(tick) mod PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP and assert the rounding loss in fee spread is bounded.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take (offer.buy=false)] Can a taker on a sell offer (offer.buy=false) where both buyerAssets and sellerAssets are rounded UP via mulDivUp cause buyerAssets - sellerAssets to be zero when units * settlementFee < WAD, so the protocol collects no settlement fee while the buyer still pays the full buyerAssets, violating the invariant that fee = buyerAssets - sellerAssets >= 0 and equals the intended spread? Proof idea: set units=1, settlementFee=1e12, buyerPrice=1e18, assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker on a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with units such that ceil(units * buyerPrice / WAD) - ceil(units * sellerPrice / WAD) > units * settlementFee / WAD cause the protocol to overcollect settlement fee, charging the buyer more than the intended spread and creating an accounting surplus in claimableSettlementFee that exceeds actual token inflows? Proof idea: fuzz units and tick, assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets <= units * settlementFee / WAD + 1.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: settlementFee / tickToPrice] Can the integer division truncation in settlementFee's interpolation formula (feeLower * (end - TTM) + feeUpper * (TTM - start)) / (end - start) produce a fee value that, when multiplied by units and divided by WAD in take, results in a claimableSettlementFee increment of zero for nonzero units, underclaiming fees? Proof idea: fuzz TTM near breakpoint boundaries and units near 1, assert fee accrual is nonzero when settlementFee > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a maker create a buy offer at tick T where tickToPrice(T) equals the current settlementFee exactly, causing sellerPrice = 0 and sellerAssets = 0, so the seller receives nothing while the buyer pays buyerAssets entirely as protocol fee, effectively charging the wrong side (buyer bears 100% of fee instead of the spread being shared)? Proof idea: find tick T where tickToPrice(T) == settlementFee, execute take, assert sellerAssets == 0 and claimableSettlementFee delta == buyerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker on a buy offer (offer.buy=true) with a tick near MAX_TICK where tickToPrice(MAX_TICK)=1e18 and settlementFee is at its maximum (cbp6 * CBP for TTM >= 360 days) cause sellerPrice = 1e18 - maxFee to be rounded to a multiple of PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP that differs from the true value, creating a fee spread that does not equal the stored settlementFeeCbp6 * CBP? Proof idea: assert tickToPrice(MAX_TICK) - settlementFee(id, 360 days) is a multiple of PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: priceToTick / tickToPrice] Can a market creator set tick spacing such that priceToTick(price, spacing) returns a tick T where tickToPrice(T) is significantly higher than price due to spacing rounding, causing the effective settlement fee spread to be computed on an inflated offerPrice, overclaiming fees from the buyer relative to the maker's intended price? Proof idea: for spacing=4 (DEFAULT_TICK_SPACING), compute max(tickToPrice(priceToTick(p, 4)) - p) and assert fee overcollection is bounded.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / buyerPendingFeeIncrease] Can a taker take a buy offer at tick 0 (tickToPrice(0)=0) where buyerPrice = 0 + settlementFee = settlementFee, causing buyerAssets = units * settlementFee / WAD (rounded down) and sellerAssets = 0, so the entire buyer payment is settlement fee, while buyerCreditIncrease = units (since buyerPos.debt=0) and buyerPendingFeeIncrease = units * continuousFee * timeToMaturity / WAD, double-charging the buyer with both settlement and continuous fees on the same units? Proof idea: assert total buyer cost = buyerAssets + future pendingFee redemption loss equals expected.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker exploit the asymmetry between mulDivDown (offer.buy=true) and mulDivUp (offer.buy=false) rounding directions to cause the settlement fee collected (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) to differ by up to 1 wei between equivalent buy and sell offers at the same tick and units, creating a systematic fee discrepancy depending on offer direction? Proof idea: for same tick, units, settlementFee, compare claimableSettlementFee delta for buy vs sell offer.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: settlementFee / tickToPrice] Can a taker time a take at TTM exactly at a breakpoint boundary (e.g., TTM = 7 days exactly) where the interpolation formula gives feeLower (cbp2 * CBP) but a take 1 second earlier gives a slightly different interpolated value, causing a discontinuity in fee collection that can be exploited by timing takes to minimize settlement fee paid? Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id, 7 days) == settlementFee(id, 7 days - 1) within 1 CBP.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / claimSettlementFee] Can a taker execute a take where buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0 (due to rounding) but the token transfer still occurs (buyerAssets > 0 to seller), causing claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] to not increase while the protocol's token balance increases by buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0, making the settlement fee permanently unclaimable for that trade? Proof idea: assert sum(claimableSettlementFee deltas) == sum(buyerAssets_i - sellerAssets_i) across all takes.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker on a buy offer where offerPrice < settlementFee cause an underflow revert in sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee (Solidity 0.8 checked arithmetic), effectively making all offers at low ticks permanently unfillable when settlement fee is high, freezing liquidity for makers who placed offers before a fee increase? Proof idea: set settlementFeeCbp6 to max, warp TTM > 360 days, attempt take on tick where tickToPrice(tick) < maxSettlementFee.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / continuousFee] Can a taker take a buy offer at TTM=0 (post-maturity, timeToMaturity=0) where buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * 0 / WAD = 0, causing the buyer to receive credit with zero pendingFee, so the protocol collects no continuous fee on post-maturity credit increases, underclaiming continuous fees? Proof idea: warp to maturity, take buy offer that reduces buyer debt to zero and creates credit, assert buyerPendingFeeIncrease == 0 and continuousFeeCredit delta == 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / _updatePosition / continuousFeeCredit] Can a lender trigger _updatePosition at block.timestamp = market.maturity where accrualEnd = min(block.timestamp, maturity) = maturity and lastAccrual < maturity, causing fee = pendingFee * (maturity - lastAccrual) / (maturity - lastAccrual) = pendingFee (full fee accrual), then immediately call _updatePosition again where lastAccrual = maturity and fee = 0, verifying that the full pendingFee is correctly transferred to continuousFeeCredit exactly once? Proof idea: assert continuousFeeCredit increases by exactly pendingFee on first call and 0 on second.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / _updatePosition] Can a lender with a large credit position and small pendingFee trigger _updatePosition at a time where (accrualEnd - lastAccrual) / (maturity - lastAccrual) rounds to zero in mulDivDown, causing accruedFee = 0 and continuousFeeCredit to not increase, underclaiming continuous fees for that period? Proof idea: fuzz lastAccrual near block.timestamp with pendingFee=1, assert accruedFee >= 0 and sum of accrued fees over full lifetime equals initial pendingFee.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / withdraw / pendingFeeDecrease] Can a lender withdraw credit in many small increments where each pendingFeeDecrease = pendingFee.mulDivUp(units, credit) rounds up, causing the total pendingFeeDecrease across all withdrawals to exceed the original pendingFee, making the final pendingFee underflow or become zero prematurely, underclaiming continuous fees from that lender? Proof idea: fuzz N partial withdrawals summing to total credit, assert sum(pendingFeeDecrease_i) <= original pendingFee.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / sellerPendingFeeDecrease] Can a taker on a sell offer where sellerPos.credit > 0 cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease = sellerPos.pendingFee.mulDivUp(sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) to round up, reducing the seller's pendingFee by more than proportional, so the seller pays less continuous fee than owed on the remaining credit, underclaiming continuous fees from the seller? Proof idea: assert sellerPos.pendingFee after take >= sellerPos.pendingFee_before * (credit_after / credit_before).

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / claimContinuousFee] Can the feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee with amount > marketState[id].withdrawable, causing an underflow revert in _marketState.withdrawable -= amount, making accumulated continuousFeeCredit permanently unclaimable if withdrawable is depleted by prior withdrawals? Proof idea: accrue continuousFeeCredit, drain withdrawable via withdraw(), attempt claimContinuousFee and assert it reverts.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / claimContinuousFee / totalUnits] Can the feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee with amount = continuousFeeCredit when totalUnits < continuousFeeCredit (possible after bad debt reduces totalUnits but continuousFeeCredit adjustment is imprecise), causing _marketState.totalUnits to underflow, corrupting market accounting? Proof idea: realize bad debt, check continuousFeeCredit vs totalUnits, attempt claimContinuousFee for full continuousFeeCredit.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take / buyerPendingFeeIncrease] Can a taker take a buy offer where continuousFee * timeToMaturity overflows uint256 before the mulDivDown in buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD), causing a revert or incorrect pendingFee assignment? Proof idea: set continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE, timeToMaturity = 100 * 365 days, assert continuousFee * timeToMaturity < type(uint256).max.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker on a buy offer where buyerCreditIncrease = 0 (buyer has enough debt to absorb all units) cause buyerPendingFeeIncrease = 0 regardless of continuousFee and timeToMaturity, so the buyer's debt reduction is not charged any continuous fee, while a lender who later receives those units via a sell offer would be charged, creating an asymmetry in continuous fee burden between debt-reducing buyers and credit-creating buyers? Proof idea: compare total continuousFeeCredit accrued for debt-reduction take vs credit-creation take with same units.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / settlementFee] Can a taker exploit the fact that settlementFee(id, TTM) for TTM >= 360 days returns a constant cbp6 * CBP, while for TTM = 359 days 23h 59m 59s the interpolation gives a value approaching but not equal to cbp6 * CBP (due to integer division), creating a discontinuity at the 360-day boundary that allows a taker to pay slightly less fee by timing the take just below 360 days? Proof idea: assert |settlementFee(id, 360 days - 1) - settlementFee(id, 360 days)| <= 1 CBP.

[File: 'src/libraries/TickLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: tickToPrice / take] Can a taker on a sell offer (offer.buy=false) where sellerPrice = offerPrice (no fee deducted from seller) and buyerPrice = offerPrice + settlementFee cause the buyer to pay more than the maker intended (offerPrice per unit) plus the settlement fee, because buyerAssets = mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) rounds up, effectively charging the buyer an extra fraction of a token that is not credited to the seller or the protocol? Proof idea: assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets == claimableSettlementFee delta and buyerAssets == sellerAssets + claimableSettlementFee delta.
